# Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) Coking Coal JM Futures — Current Price Report

## Key Findings

### JM2609 (September 2026 Delivery) — Most Active Contract
As of **June 1, 2026**, the JM2609 contract is the most actively traded coking coal futures contract on the DCE, with the following data:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Close Price** | **¥1,377.0/tonne** |
| **Open** | ¥1,288.0 |
| **Day High** | ¥1,387.5 |
| **Day Low** | ¥1,287.0 |
| **Settlement** | ¥1,346.5 |
| **Volume** | 1,562,733 lots |
| **Open Interest** | 547,028 lots |

This represents a **significant rally** on June 1, with an intraday range of ¥100.5 (¥1,287.0–¥1,387.5) and a close of ¥1,377.0 — up **¥91.5 (+7.1%)** from the previous close of ¥1,285.5 on May 29. Volume surged to 1.56 million lots, more than double the prior session.

### JM2606 (June 2026 Delivery) — Near-Expiry Contract
The JM2606 contract, which is nearing expiration, traded with much thinner volume:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Close Price** | **¥1,190.5/tonne** |
| **Open** | ¥1,112.5 |
| **Day High** | ¥1,200.0 |
| **Day Low** | ¥1,112.5 |
| **Volume** | 143 lots |
| **Open Interest** | 234 lots |

JM2606 closed up **¥80.0 (+7.2%)** from its prior close of ¥1,110.5 on May 29. Note the very low liquidity — only 143 lots traded — typical for a near-expiry contract.

### JM0 (Continuous/Front-Month Contract)
The continuous contract (JM0) mirrors JM2609 as the most-active month:
- **Close (June 1):** ¥1,377.0
- **Previous close (May 29):** ¥1,285.5

## Recent Price Trajectory (JM2609)

The JM2609 contract has shown significant volatility in late May and early June 2026:

| Date | Close (¥/t) | Change |
|---|---|---|
| May 12 | 1,241.0 | -68.0 (-5.2%) |
| May 15 | 1,225.0 | — |
| May 18 | 1,208.5 | -16.5 |
| May 22 | 1,162.5 | -28.0 |
| May 25 | 1,266.5 | +104.0 (+8.9%) |
| May 26 | 1,260.0 | -6.5 |
| May 27 | 1,273.0 | +13.0 |
| May 28 | 1,290.0 | +17.0 |
| May 29 | 1,285.5 | -4.5 |
| **June 1** | **1,377.0** | **+91.5 (+7.1%)** |

The contract hit a recent low of ¥1,152.5 (intraday on May 25) before staging a dramatic **V-shaped recovery**, rallying approximately **19.5%** from trough to the June 1 close. The May 25–June 1 period shows exceptionally high volatility and surging volumes.

### Longer-Term Context (April–June 2026)
- **April range:** ~¥1,210–1,298.5
- **Early May:** Drifted from ~1,287 to ~1,313.5, then declined sharply
- **Mid-May decline:** Fell from ~1,309 to ~1,162.5 (peak-to-trough ~11%)
- **Late May recovery:** Bounced sharply back above ¥1,260
- **June 1:** Surged to ¥1,377.0 — highest level since at least early April

## Data Source Notes
- **Source:** Sina Finance / sina.com.cn futures data API (InnerFuturesNewService)
- **Data fields:** d=date, o=open, h=high, l=low, c=close, v=volume, p=open interest, s=settlement
- **Currency:** Chinese Yuan (CNY/RMB) per metric tonne
- **Exchange:** Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE)
- **Contract size:** 60 tonnes per lot
- **Access date:** June 1, 2026
